rulings.Case Title: ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION, A NON-PROFIT VS. CALIFORNIA STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD, A ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 18, 2025, Line 1. 2 - RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD MOTION TO STRIKE. Continued on the court's own motion to be heard January 6, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: TREVOR NEADEAU VS. BROADWAY MANAGEMENT COMPANY,LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 18, 2025, Line 8. COMPLIANCE HEARING. On calendar is a Settlement Compliance Hearing (PAGA Settlement). HEARING REQUIRED. The court orders Plaintiff's counsel to show cause why they should not be held in contempt or ordered to pay $500 in sanctions to the clerk of the court or both for violating the court's April 29, 2025 Order. Specifically, on April 29, 2025, the court ordered "Plaintiff's counsel to file a declaration re disposition of settlement proceeds and any other outstanding issues that may be discussed at the hearing no later than December 8, 2025, with courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing." (4/29/2025 Order at p. 1.) Plaintiff's counsel did neither. The OSC will be heard on March 4, 2026, at 9 am in this department. Plaintiff's counsel of record's personal appearance is required at the OSC hearing. Any response to the OSC must be filed no later than February 18, 2025, with courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing. The Settlement Compliance Hearing (PAGA Settlement) is continued to March 4, 2026, at 9 am in this department. Plaintiff's counsel must file a declaration re disposition of settlement proceeds and any other outstanding issues that may be discussed at the hearing no later than February 18, 2025, with courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing. Plaintiff is ordered to submit a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on December 18, 2025. Plaintiff shall send the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY LEVI ET AL VS. BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 18, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANT BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendant Barclay's Bank Delaware's unopposed Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Plaintiffs are ordered to submit this dispute to arbitration under the Barclay's cardmember agreement. (Beauchamp Decl., exs. G & H.) The record demonstrates the parties entered an arbitration agreement and the agreement appears to cover the disputes at issue. Notably, Plaintiffs failed to oppose the motion, and the court deems the absence of an opposition as consent to the granting of the motion. (See California Rules of Court, rule 8.54(c).) Plaintiffs' claims against Barclay's are ordered to arbitration and the matter is stayed pending the outcome of the arbitration proceedings. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
